title: Vegan scones
subtitle: Plain scones made without dairy or eggs
excerpt: Light, well-risen scones made with plant-based butter and oat milk, without eggs or dairy. The technique is identical to a standard buttermilk scone: cold fat rubbed into flour, minimal mixing, high heat.
sourceNotes: Vegan scones are a modern adaptation of the classic British plain scone. The technique is identical: rubbing fat into flour, adding liquid minimally, baking at high heat. Plant-based block butter and oat milk are the replacements; block plant butter gives a better crumb than soft spread. Oat milk is used for its neutral flavour and milk-like fat content. The small amount of apple cider vinegar acidifies the oat milk to approximate buttermilk.

content.text: Vegan scones are made exactly like dairy scones; only the fat and liquid change. Plant-based block butter must be used cold and straight from the refrigerator, just as dairy butter would be. The oat milk is acidified with a small amount of vinegar to mimic buttermilk. Apply the
lightly to get the best rise.

Preheat the oven to 220°C conventional. Line a baking tray with baking paper. Stir the vinegar into the oat milk and set aside for 2 minutes; it will curdle slightly. Sift the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar into a large bowl. Rub in the cold plant butter until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Make a well and pour in the acidified oat milk. Stir briefly with a knife until the dough just comes together. Turn onto a lightly floured surface, pat to 2.5 cm thick, and cut rounds with a 6 cm cutter, pressing straight down. Place on the tray. Brush the tops lightly with a little extra oat milk. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until well risen and golden brown. Cool on a rack.
